_comment: LAIT Test Prompts - Varying sizes for testing compression and reconstruction
_description: Test prompts organized by size category. Use with verify_adapter.py or lait_mcp_server.py

medium_1.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

medium_2.text: Machine learning is transforming technology worldwide.

medium_3.text: Neural networks can learn complex patterns from data.

medium_4.text: The adapter compresses text into latent representations.

medium_5.text: Genetic evolution finds optimal architecture configurations.

long_1.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This is a longer sentence that tests the adapter ability to reconstruct arbitrary text accurately.

long_2.text: Machine learning is transforming technology worldwide. Neural networks can learn complex patterns from data and make predictions about future events.

long_3.text: The adapter compresses text into latent representations using a transformer encoder-decoder architecture with adaptive pooling bottleneck.

long_4.text: Genetic evolution explores a 120-trait genome to find optimal architecture configurations for neural text compression with 100% reconstruction.

long_5.text: Los Angeles (US), Jun 20 (IANS) A massive wildfire has broken out in the hills above Los Angeles, forcing thousands of residents to evacuate their homes.

technical_1.text: def train_model(config, data): model = LAITAdapter(config); optimizer = AdamW(model.parameters()); for epoch in range(100): loss = train_epoch(model, data); return model

technical_3.text: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 25 AND city = "Los Angeles" ORDER BY name ASC LIMIT 100

technical_4.text: git commit -m "feat: add GPU training support with CUDA 12.8 for RTX 5060"

sentence_1.text: The system achieves 100% reconstruction accuracy at all compression ratios from 1x to 16x on GPU.

sentence_2.text: LAIT integrates with Ollama language models via a Model Context Protocol server for real-time compression.

sentence_3.text: The 120-trait genome defines architecture depth, model dimensions, compression parameters, and bottleneck types.

sentence_4.text: Training uses AdamW optimizer with cosine annealing scheduler and cross-entropy loss on 500 diverse samples.

sentence_5.text: The MCP server exposes 5 tools: compress, decompress, list, stats, and clear for managing text compression.

paragraph_1.text: LAIT (Latent Attention in Tokens) is a neural text compression system that achieves 100% lossless reconstruction of arbitrary text through learned latent representations. The system integrates with Ollama language models via a Model Context Protocol server, enabling real-time compression and decompression as a tool service.

paragraph_2.text: The adapter is trained using genetic evolution across a 120-trait genome to find optimal architecture configurations. The genome defines architecture depth, model dimensions, compression parameters, bottleneck types, attention mechanisms, training hyperparameters, and evolution strategy. This approach achieves 100% reconstruction accuracy at compression ratios up to 16x on GPU.

paragraph_3.text: The MCP server exposes 5 tools for managing text compression: lait_compress compresses text to latent representation, lait_decompress decompresses latent back to text, lait_list lists all cached compressions, lait_stats gets compression statistics, and lait_clear clears the compression cache. The server runs on port 8001 and supports both API and MCP protocol access.

usage.test_single: python -c "from verify_adapter import *; adapter, config, _ = load_adapter(); print(test_reconstruction(adapter, 'Hello world!'))"
usage.test_compression: python -c "from verify_adapter import *; adapter, config, _ = load_adapter(); print(test_compression(adapter, 'Hello world!', 0.5))"
